hypothesis:
Given that PCDiff enforces physics constraints only during the diffusion training process but does not explicitly model the complex interplay between manufacturing process parameters (e.g., curing temperature, pressure, fiber orientation) and final composite properties, I hypothesize that:

**Incorporating manufacturing process parameters as additional conditional inputs to PCDiff, alongside target material properties, will enable the generation of composite designs that are not only physically valid and property-optimized but also manufacturing-feasible, thereby reducing the gap between computationally designed compositions and experimentally realizable materials.**
